short_meanings: a level of authority or status
hero.one_line_summary: An echelon is either a level within a hierarchy or a formation arranged in a series of offset positions.
hero.memorable_example: The proposal must be approved by the upper echelons of management.

meanings.definition: a particular level of authority, importance, or status within an organization, profession, or society
examples: The proposal must be approved by the upper echelons of management.
Women remain underrepresented in the highest echelons of the industry.
Information often moves slowly between different echelons of a large organization.
Only officials in the top echelon had access to the confidential report.
The reforms affected employees at every echelon of the institution.
He gradually rose through the echelons of the diplomatic service.
The researcher interviewed people from several social echelons.
meanings.typical_pattern: the upper, higher, or top echelons of + organization or field
meanings.register: formal; common in business, politics, sociology, and institutional discussions

nuance.central_nuance: The word presents an organization or society as a hierarchy with several levels.
nuance.usual_implication: It is especially common when discussing people with high authority, influence, or status.
nuance.contrast_explanation: Rank usually refers to the official position of one person. Echelon refers more broadly to a whole level or group within a hierarchy.

grammar.how_to_use: Use an echelon for one level and echelons for several levels.
grammar.what_can_follow: It is often followed by of and the name of an organization, profession, industry, or society.
grammar.preposition_note: Use in when describing someone within a level, as in people in the highest echelons of government.
grammar.noun_use: This is a countable noun. The plural form is especially common in expressions such as the upper echelons and the highest echelons.

patterns.pattern: the upper echelons of + organization or field
patterns.use: Use this for the people at the highest and most powerful levels.
patterns.example: The policy was debated within the upper echelons of the company.

patterns.use: Use this for one highest level of authority, ability, or status.
patterns.example: Few athletes ever reach the top echelon of international competition.

patterns.use: Use this when something affects or involves all levels.
patterns.example: The new safety rules apply at every echelon of the organization.

patterns.pattern: rise through the echelons of + profession
patterns.use: Use this when someone gradually moves to increasingly important positions.
patterns.example: She rose through the echelons of the civil service.

errors.wrong: The decision was made by the high echelon of the company.
errors.correct: The decision was made by the upper echelons of the company.
errors.explanation: Upper echelons is the usual expression for the most powerful levels of an organization.

errors.wrong: He works on the highest echelon of government.
errors.correct: He works in the highest echelon of government.
errors.explanation: Use in when referring to someone who belongs to a particular level.

errors.wrong: The company has several different echelon levels.
errors.correct: The company has several different echelons.
errors.explanation: Echelon already means a level, so echelon level is usually unnecessary.

meanings.definition: a military formation in which each group of soldiers, vehicles, ships, or aircraft is positioned behind and to one side of the group ahead
examples: The aircraft approached the target in an echelon formation.
The ships moved in echelon to maintain a clear view of one another.
The soldiers advanced in echelon across the open ground.
The vehicles formed a right echelon before entering the valley.
An echelon arrangement allowed each unit to support the one beside it.
The pilots practiced changing from a straight line into an echelon.
The commander ordered the troops to move forward in a left echelon.
meanings.plain_english_equivalent: a diagonal line of military units
meanings.typical_pattern: in echelon or an echelon formation
meanings.register: specialized but common in military and historical descriptions

nuance.central_nuance: The units are not directly beside or behind one another; each is offset to the left or right.
nuance.usual_implication: This arrangement can improve visibility, coordination, or the ability of units to support one another.

grammar.how_to_use: Use an echelon for the formation itself. The expression in echelon describes how units are arranged or moving.
grammar.what_can_follow: The noun is commonly used before formation, or after words such as form, move, advance, and fly.
grammar.position_note: In the phrase echelon formation, echelon comes before the noun formation.

patterns.use: Use this to name the diagonal arrangement.
patterns.example: The helicopters flew in an echelon formation.

patterns.pattern: move, advance, or fly in echelon
patterns.use: Use this to describe units traveling in the formation.
patterns.example: The armored vehicles advanced in echelon.

patterns.use: Use left or right to show the direction in which the formation extends.
patterns.example: The squadron shifted into a right echelon.

errors.explanation: Use in echelon to describe movement in this formation.

errors.wrong: The troops formed into an echelon formation.
errors.explanation: Form an echelon is more direct. You can also say moved into an echelon formation.

collocations.example: The scandal reached the upper echelons of the administration.
collocations.meaning: the highest and most powerful levels
collocations.grammar_pattern: the upper echelons of + organization or field

collocations.example: Graduates of the program have entered the highest echelons of public service.
collocations.meaning: the levels with the greatest authority, influence, or status
collocations.grammar_pattern: the highest echelons of + organization, profession, or society

collocations.example: The university belongs to the top echelon of research institutions.
collocations.meaning: the highest level of quality, power, or status
collocations.grammar_pattern: the top echelon of + field or group

collocations.example: The policy was unpopular among workers in the lower echelons of the organization.
collocations.meaning: levels with less authority or status

collocations.example: The investigation revealed problems at every echelon of the institution.
The aircraft maintained an echelon formation during the exercise.
collocations.meaning: a formation in which each unit is behind and to one side of another

collocations.example: The ships moved in echelon along the coast.
collocations.meaning: travel while arranged in a diagonal formation
collocations.grammar_pattern: move, advance, or fly in echelon

collocations.example: The vehicles formed a right echelon as they approached the bridge.
collocations.meaning: a formation extending diagonally toward the right

synonyms.difference: Level is a broad everyday word. Echelon is more formal and emphasizes a position within an organized hierarchy.
synonyms.example: The issue must be discussed at the highest level of government.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both words can refer to a position within a hierarchy.

synonyms.difference: Tier commonly describes one of several clearly separated levels in a system, service, or structure. Echelon is especially associated with authority, status, or organizational hierarchy.
synonyms.example: Customers can choose from three different service tiers.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both words describe one level in a structure containing several levels.

synonyms.difference: Rank often identifies the official position of a particular person. Echelon usually describes a broader level containing several people.
synonyms.example: She achieved the rank of senior officer.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both can indicate a position within a hierarchy.

synonyms.difference: Formation is a general word for any organized arrangement of military units. Echelon refers to one specific diagonal arrangement.
synonyms.example: The pilots maintained a tight formation.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both words can describe an organized arrangement of soldiers, vehicles, ships, or aircraft.

etymology.origin: The word entered English from French échelon, meaning a rung of a ladder or a step.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The policy was approved by officials in the upper echelons of government.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: The upper echelons of is a natural expression for the highest levels of authority.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: She works on the highest echelon of the organization.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: She works in the highest echelon of the organization.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Use in when someone belongs to a particular echelon.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The company has four separate echelon levels.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: The company has four separate echelons.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Echelon already means level, so adding levels is redundant.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The aircraft flew in echelon during the military exercise.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: In echelon correctly describes movement in a diagonal formation.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The investigation involved officials in the upper _____ of the ministry.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The investigation involved officials in the upper echelons of the ministry.
collocation_practice.explanation: The upper echelons is the standard expression for the most powerful levels.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: Very few musicians reach the top _____ of the profession.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: Very few musicians reach the top echelon of the profession.
collocation_practice.explanation: Top echelon means the highest level of achievement or status.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The helicopters flew in an echelon _____ during the demonstration.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The helicopters flew in an echelon formation during the demonstration.
collocation_practice.explanation: Echelon formation is the established term for this diagonal arrangement.
